sections.heading: Problem Statement
sections.content:
The graph view shows every indexed node at once — which is useful for small projects but becomes an undifferentiated blob for anything real. Developers cannot tell what they're looking at because there's no *context* for why these nodes are on screen. The graph should be a magnifying glass, not a dump of the entire index.

Three specific problems:

**No entry context.** The graph loads all nodes by default. For large projects (>400 nodes) it falls back to list view, but even for medium projects (100-400 nodes) the all-nodes view is overwhelming. There is no workflow for "show me just these 15 stale functions and how they connect" — the developer must mentally filter while staring at a hairball.

**Tags render as tiny chips, not meaningful content.** Tag chips on graph nodes are small colored pills — fine for the list view but wasted in the graph where there's space for richer rendering. The docs view renders structured content beautifully; tags in the graph should follow the same pattern — small cards or labels that are readable without hovering.

**No validation actions.** The graph shows staleness rings but provides no way to *act* on them. A developer sees an orange ring, clicks the node, reads the detail panel, and then has to switch to the CLI to verify it. The graph should support right-click → verify and visual feedback when a node transitions from stale to verified.

**Stale count is invisible on initial load.** The sidebar shows total node count but no aggregate staleness summary. A developer cannot tell at a glance how many nodes need attention without applying filters one by one.

sections.heading: User Stories
sections.content:
**US-GV-01 — See a focused subgraph with health indicators**
As a developer, I want the graph view to show a scoped subset of nodes (from list selection, search, or neighborhood) with type-colored fills and staleness-colored outlines so I can visually understand the structure and health of the nodes I'm investigating.

**US-GV-02 — Distinguish node type from staleness visually**
As a developer, I want node fill color (type) and node outline color (staleness) to use clearly distinct color palettes so I can tell at a glance both what kind of node it is and whether it's stale — without the two signals blending together.

**US-GV-03 — Select layout algorithm**
As a developer, I want to choose between layout algorithms (cose, dagre, breadthfirst, grid, concentric) so I can pick the layout that best reveals the current subgraph's structure.

**US-GV-04 — Filter by edge type**
As a developer, I want to toggle edge types (composes, calls, validates, documents, delegates_to) so I can see only the dependency relationships relevant to my current task.

**US-GV-05 — Rescan without leaving the graph**
As a developer, I want a Rescan button that triggers a cortex build and refreshes the graph in place so I can see the updated staleness state after editing code.

**US-GV-06 — Re-check staleness without rebuilding**
As a developer, I want a Check button that re-reads source files and updates staleness rings in place so I can see whether code I just edited is stale without waiting for a full rebuild.

**US-GV-07 — Graph opens with context, not everything**
As a developer, I want the graph to always open with a context — selected list nodes, a search result, or a specific node's neighborhood — so that I see a focused subgraph relevant to my task instead of every node in the project.

**US-GV-08 — Spawn graph from list selection**
As a developer, I want to select nodes in the list view and click "View in Graph" to see a graph scoped to those nodes plus their connecting edges (with configurable 1-hop expansion) so that the graph shows exactly what I'm investigating.

**US-GV-09 — Graph breadcrumbs**
As a developer, I want the graph to show a breadcrumb indicating its current context (e.g., "12 nodes from filter: staleness=CONTENT_STALE, type=function") with a click-to-return link to the list with the same filter, so I can navigate between list and graph without losing my place.

**US-GV-10 — Tags as doc-style cards**
As a developer, I want tags on graph nodes to render as readable card-style labels (similar to how the docs view renders section headers) instead of tiny chips, so I can read tag information without hovering or clicking.

**US-GV-11 — Verify nodes from the graph**
As a developer, I want to right-click a node and select "Mark Verified" (with optional reason) so I can act on staleness directly in the graph. Verified nodes should get a green checkmark overlay and their ring should update in place.

**US-GV-12 — Staleness heatmap mode**
As a developer, I want a toggle to color nodes by staleness severity instead of type (red=structural drift, orange=content stale, yellow=desc stale, green=clean/verified) so I can see the health distribution at a glance.

**US-GV-13 — Expand/collapse composite nodes**
As a developer, I want to double-click a composite node to expand it inline (showing its children as nested nodes) or collapse it back so I can drill into module structure without loading a separate neighborhood view.

sections.heading: Current Capabilities
sections.content:
| Capability | Status |
|---|---|
| Cytoscape.js graph render with nodes and edges | Done |
| Node fill coloring by subtype (function=green, module=blue, docjson=orange, test=red, entity=purple, external_package=grey) | Done |
| Staleness rings on nodes (CONTENT_STALE=blue, DESC_STALE=orange, LINKED_STALE=yellow, STRUCTURAL_DRIFT=red, VERIFIED=green) | Done |
| Edge type toggle checkboxes | Done |
| Layout selector — cose, dagre, breadthfirst, grid, concentric | Done |
| Layout animation disabled (instant placement) | Done |
| Rescan button (POST /api/rescan + graph refresh) | Done |
| Check button (hash-based staleness refresh via shared engine, writes to DB) | Done |
| Large project mode (>400 nodes → defaults to list view) | Done |
| Step edge labels toggle (dFlow step numbers) | Done |
| Node click → neighborhood graph + detail panel | Done |
| Stale count summary in sidebar (N content stale · N linked stale) | Done |
| `/api/all` returns persisted staleness column — no background refresh needed | Done |
| Hub node sizing (high in-degree >3 → larger nodes) | Done |
| Hide orphan nodes toggle | Done |
| Distinct fill vs outline color palettes (type vs staleness never confusable) (US-GV-02) | Not started |
| Context-driven graph launch (graph opens from list selection, not all-nodes) (US-GV-07) | Not started |
| Spawn graph from list filter/selection (US-GV-08) | Not started |
| Graph breadcrumbs with context label and return-to-list link (US-GV-09) | Not started |
| Tags as doc-style cards instead of tiny chips (US-GV-10) | Not started |
| Right-click verify on graph nodes (US-GV-11) | Not started |
| Staleness heatmap mode toggle (US-GV-12) | Not started |
| Expand/collapse composite nodes inline (US-GV-13) | Not started |
| `POST /api/subgraph` endpoint for list→graph node set | Not started |

sections.heading: Staleness Count Discrepancy
sections.content:
~~The two-tier staleness problem (fast mtime path vs rich hash path) has been resolved by ADR-009.~~ `/api/all` now reads the persisted `staleness` column directly — no background `_refreshStaleness` needed. The Check button recomputes via the shared staleness engine and writes back to DB. No more transient `NEEDS_RESCAN` state.

**Remaining gap: doc section inflation.** `cortex check` counts all node types including doc sections. In many projects, the majority of stale nodes are doc sections whose linked code changed. These are valid in the graph but cluster visually. The staleness summary widget helps (breaks down by category: code/doc/test) but the graph itself still renders doc sections the same size as functions. Future: composite expand/collapse (US-GV-16) would let doc file nodes absorb their sections visually."

sections.heading: Backlog
sections.content:
**Context-driven graph launch (US-GV-10, US-GV-11)** — The graph should never open with "all nodes" as default. When switching to graph view: (1) if nodes are selected in list → show those nodes + connecting edges, (2) if a filter is active → show filtered nodes, (3) if a node was last selected → show its neighborhood, (4) fallback → show a "pick a node or filter first" prompt. The graph tab remains directly clickable (it's not removed), but it always opens with *context*. For small projects (<50 nodes), "show all" is still fine as an auto-context. New `POST /api/subgraph` endpoint accepts `{node_ids: [...], expand_hops: 1}` and returns the subgraph.

**Graph breadcrumbs (US-GV-12)** — A breadcrumb bar at the top of the graph canvas showing the current context: "12 nodes from filter: staleness=CONTENT_STALE, type=function" or "Neighborhood of cortex.index.db::upsert_node (depth 2)". Clicking the breadcrumb returns to the list with the same filter applied. Breadcrumb history (stack) allows multi-level drill-down.

**Tags as doc-style cards (US-GV-13)** — Replace the current tiny `.tag-chip` pills on graph nodes with card-style labels similar to how the docs view renders section headers. Each tag becomes a small rectangular label with readable font size, positioned below the node label. For nodes with >3 tags, show first 3 + "+N more" expandable on hover. Uses the same color scheme as tag pills but with padding and a subtle background.

**Right-click verify (US-GV-14)** — Add a context menu (Cytoscape `cxttap` event) on graph nodes with options: "Mark Verified" (with reason prompt), "View in List", "Expand Neighborhood". On verify: calls `POST /api/nodes/{id}/verify`, updates the node's staleness ring from stale color to green with a brief pulse animation. If the staleness cause metadata is available, show the cause in the context menu tooltip.

**Staleness heatmap mode (US-GV-15)** — A toggle button in the graph toolbar switches node coloring from type-based (current) to staleness-based. In heatmap mode: STRUCTURAL_DRIFT=red, CONTENT_STALE=orange, DESC_STALE=yellow, LINKED_STALE=light orange, VERIFIED=green, CLEAN=gray. Edge colors remain unchanged. The toggle is persistent in session storage.

**Expand/collapse composite nodes (US-GV-16)** — Double-click a `composite_process` node to expand it into a compound Cytoscape node showing its children (via `composes` edges) as nested nodes. Double-click again to collapse. Uses Cytoscape's compound node feature (`parent` data field). The expanded state shows children with their own type colors and staleness rings. This addresses the doc section visual clustering problem — a doc file node can be expanded to show its sections or collapsed to a single node.

**Focus mode (US-GV-17)** — Double-click any node to enter focus mode: hide all nodes except the clicked node's N-hop neighborhood (depth configurable via the existing neighborhood slider). Show a "Focused on: {node name}" breadcrumb with an "Exit focus" button. Focus is stackable — double-clicking a neighbor enters deeper focus with breadcrumb trail.

**Stale node explanation in detail panel (US-GV-05)** — When a stale node is selected, the detail panel shows a "Staleness Cause" section explaining *why*: which hash field changed (code, desc, or both), which linked node triggered LINKED_STALE (with clickable link to that node), which children caused composite inheritance (with expandable child list). Requires the staleness cause metadata from the staleness PRD (US-ST-13).

**Staleness count per filter option** — In the sidebar staleness radio list, show counts per status (e.g. 'CONTENT_STALE (3)'). Makes it clear how many nodes match each filter without having to apply it.
